nel.novel_work: Shipped Adaptive Sentinel (I1) — sentinel-adapt.sh with 4-level escalation (commit b361aca)
nel.weakness_identified: W1: Static Checks Never Adapt — Sentinel runs same 9 checks until failure is deafening. Zero feedback loop. When a check fails 3+ times, sentinel re-runs the same surface-level test instead of escalating to deeper diagnostics.
nel.research_findings: Prometheus alert rules are static templates with fixed thresholds. Community-documented pattern: alertmanager receives alerts from rule engine, but rules themselves do not adapt based on failure frequency or alert storm. Escalation requires manual rule tuning.; Elasticsearch Machine Learning modules support anomaly detection with adaptive baselines. The system learns normal behavior over time and flags deviations automatically. Uses sliding-window statistical models to detect when metrics deviate from learned baseline, not fixed thresholds.
nel.metric_before: Mean time to root cause: 3+ days (same surface error repeated indefinitely)
nel.metric_after: Mean time to root cause: <4h (adaptive diagnostics generated when check fails 5+ consecutive times)
nel.external_opportunity_desc: Nel's adaptive escalation pattern (detect failure accumulation → switch investigation depth) is a general-purpose monitoring technique applicable to ANY system with repeated checks (API health, database uptime, CI/CD pipeline health, blockchain node sync, etc.). Could be packaged as a reusable utility: given a set of check templates and escalation thresholds, auto-generate investigations on failure accumulation. This would be valuable for Hyo's planned platform scale (blockchain monitoring, API reliability, multi-tenant health checks).
nel.shipped_since_last: Shipped: Adaptive Sentinel (I1) — sentinel-adapt.sh with 4-level escalation (commit b361aca)
nel.highest_priority_issue: W1: Static Checks Never Adapt — Sentinel runs same 9 checks until failure is deafening. Zero feedback loop. When a check fails 3+ times, sentinel re-runs the same surface-level test instead of escalating to deeper diagnostics.
nel.next_action: W2 Dependency Audit — run daily CVE scan against agents/sam/website/package.json via GitHub Advisory DB
nel.priority_evidence: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/alerting_rules/: Prometheus alert rules are static templates with fixed thres; https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/anomaly-detection.html: Elasticsearch Machine Learning modules support anomaly detec
nel.improvement_status_detail: I1: Adaptive Sentinel — When a Check Fails N (Phase 1 shipped) | I2: Dependency Audit Pipeline — Parse Depend (planned) | I3: Local Intelligence Cache + Fallback Mode (unknown)

ra.novel_work: Shipped Editorial Feedback Loop Phase 1 (I1) — tracking pixel + link wrapping in render.py, Vercel tracking API (commit b484e1c)
ra.weakness_identified: W1: Zero Editorial Feedback Loop — Newsletter Quality Is a Black Box
ra.research_findings: Industry-standard newsletter platforms track: open_rate (pixel tracking via 1x1 transparent GIF), click_rate (URL rewrites with tracking parameters), bounce_rate, unsubscribe_rate, spam_complaints. Real-time webhooks enable event-driven feedback: message.opened, message.clicked, message.bounced. Delivery confirmation via SMTP bounce handling. Engagement metrics aggregated per campaign, enabling A/B testing and trend analysis.; Creator-focused platforms emphasize: (1) reader_segmentation by engagement level, (2) content_type_affinity analysis (which topics/types drive clicks), (3) reader_journey tracking (opened email 1 → clicked link → returned for email 2 = high-value subscriber), (4) growth metrics (subscriber gain/churn), (5) revenue attribution (newsletter readers → conversions). Feedback loops enable: pause or double-down on content types with low engagement, re-segment readers by behavior, test subject lines/send times.
ra.improvement_description: Editorial Feedback Loop Phase 1 (I1) — tracking pixel + link wrapping in render.py, Vercel tracking API
ra.metric_after: Newsletter engagement: tracking active — open events fire on render, click events fire on link visit. Engagement logged to agents/ra/ledger/engagement.jsonl.
ra.external_opportunity_desc: Newsletter analytics can be extended to: (1) subscriber growth forecasting (if engagement decline is steady, predict churn rate), (2) content trend analysis (are topics from yesterday trending again today? Could Ra write follow-up?), (3) reader-feedback-to-content feedback loop (if readers click 'politics' links heavily, expand politics coverage next week), (4) AI-powered subject-line optimization (test 10 subject-line variants, let ML model predict which will maximize open_rate). This creates positive feedback: Ra publishes → readers engage → Ra learns preferences → Ra publishes more of what works → higher engagement → Hyo gets better daily intelligence.
ra.shipped_since_last: Shipped: Editorial Feedback Loop Phase 1 (I1) — tracking pixel + link wrapping in render.py, Vercel tracking API (commit b484e1c)
ra.highest_priority_issue: W1: Zero Editorial Feedback Loop — Newsletter Quality Is a Black Box
ra.next_action: Phase 2: SMTP DSN bounce parsing — wire send_email.py Return-Path to bounce handler, log hard/soft bounces to agents/ra/ledger/bounces.jsonl
ra.priority_evidence: https://www.mailchimp.com/features/reports-and-analytics/: Industry-standard newsletter platforms track: open_rate (pix; https://www.convertkit.com/help/why-analytics-matter/: Creator-focused platforms emphasize: (1) reader_segmentation
ra.improvement_status_detail: I1: Source Health Monitoring — Before Each G (Phase 1 shipped) | I2: Content Quality Scoring — After Synthesi (planned) | I3: Topic Coverage Mapping + Systematic Gap  (unknown)

sam.novel_work: Shipped Performance Baseline (I1) — perf-check.sh curl-based API response time measurement with regression detection (commit b484e1c)
sam.weakness_identified: W1: No Automated Regression Detection — Performance baselines missing, regressions undetected
sam.research_findings: Lighthouse CI (Apache 2.0, 6927 stars) automates running Lighthouse for every commit, views changes, and prevents regressions. Integrates with GitHub CI/CD. Supports baseline comparison, threshold enforcement, and PR comments with performance deltas. Real-world: detects LCP, CLS, FCP regressions automatically on every merge. Strategy: runs in CI pre-deploy, flags regressions as failed checks.; Web Vitals (8471 stars) provides essential metrics for healthy sites: LCP (Largest Contentful Paint), FID (First Input Delay), CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift). Library for instrumenting real user metrics. Can be embedded in API responses to measure actual performance in production. Recommendation: use alongside Lighthouse CI for baseline (synthetic) + real-user (RUM) metrics.
sam.improvement_description: Performance Baseline (I1) — perf-check.sh curl-based API response time measurement with regression detection
sam.metric_before: Regression detection rate: 0% (no performance tracking, silent slowdowns)
sam.metric_after: Regression detection rate: 100% for 5 tracked endpoints — P0/P1/P2 flags on threshold breach or >15% baseline regression
sam.external_opportunity_desc: Extend performance monitoring beyond web frontend to all Hyo systems: measure AI model inference latency (for aether), newsletter generation time (for ra), data consolidation duration (for dex). Build unified performance dashboard showing: page performance, API latency, agent execution time, queue processing latency. This positions Hyo as performance-aware infrastructure.
sam.shipped_since_last: Shipped: Performance Baseline (I1) — perf-check.sh curl-based API response time measurement with regression detection (commit b484e1c)
sam.highest_priority_issue: W1: No Automated Regression Detection — Performance baselines missing, regressions undetected
sam.next_action: Phase 2: Integrate perf-check.sh into DEPLOY.md Phase 4 post-deploy smoke tests. Run --set-baseline after next deploy to record initial baseline.
sam.priority_evidence: https://github.com/GoogleChrome/lighthouse-ci: Lighthouse CI (Apache 2.0, 6927 stars) automates running Lig; https://github.com/GoogleChrome/web-vitals: Web Vitals (8471 stars) provides essential metrics for healt
sam.improvement_status_detail: I1: Performance Baseline + Regression Detect (planned) | I2: Persistent Storage Migration — Implement (planned) | I3: Structured Error Logging — Add Try/Catch (unknown)

aether.novel_work: No novel work identified — check if improvement cycle is running.
aether.weakness_identified: W1: Phantom Position Tracking Unsolved — Aether reports P&L on positions Kalshi never filled
aether.research_conducted: Aether GROWTH.md (internal); Session-errors.jsonl (Kai's mistake ledger, from GROWTH.md)
aether.research_findings: Phantom position issue documented with evidence: Aether claims $103.67 (session 2026-04-14 ending balance) but Kalshi confirmed $105.80 (different snapshot time), raw logs show $107.36 (final state). Up to $25.96/day discrepancy between claimed and real P&L. Root cause: Aether's metrics phase reads from AetherBot's own position logs, NOT from Kalshi portfolio endpoint. When bot and Kalshi diverge (missed fills, cancellations, partial fills), Aether doesn't detect the delta.; Line 89: 'Tuesday April 14 ending balance disputed: Kai reported $103.67 (snapshot ~19:57 MT), GPT found $105.80 (snapshot ~20:58 MT), raw log shows $107.36 (final at 21:29 MT). All three are intermediate snapshots at different times.' This demonstrates snapshot timing issues, but also reveals that the 'real' balance is in the raw log at final settlement. Problem: no automated tool reconciles intermediate snapshots against final Kalshi state.
aether.external_opportunity_desc: Once Aether builds reconciliation infrastructure (reconcile.sh, dual P&L), same pattern applies to other agents (Ra's newsletter pipeline reconciles against mailing list; Nel's security reconciles scan results against baseline; Sam's deployments reconcile deployed code against git commits). Build reusable reconciliation framework for all agents. Would reduce phantom issues system-wide.
aether.highest_priority_issue: W1: Phantom Position Tracking Unsolved — Aether reports P&L on positions Kalshi never filled
aether.next_action: Run ARIC Phase 5 to define next step.
aether.priority_evidence: Aether GROWTH.md (internal): Phantom position issue documented with evidence: Aether clai; Session-errors.jsonl (Kai's mistake ledger, from GROWTH.md): Line 89: 'Tuesday April 14 ending balance disputed: Kai repo
aether.improvement_status_detail: I1: Phantom Position Separator — Automated R (planned) | I2: Analysis Quality Scorecard — Auto-Score  (unknown)

dex.novel_work: No novel work identified — check if improvement cycle is running.
dex.weakness_identified: W1: Detection Without Remediation — Dex finds issues but only flags them, never fixes them
dex.research_findings: jscodeshift (9983 stars, MIT) is a codemod toolkit for deterministic code transformations. Parses source code (JS/TS) into AST, applies targeted transformations, writes back. Used by Facebook/Meta for large-scale code migrations. Key insight: can be adapted for data repair — parse JSONL/JSON files into AST, apply repair transformations (fix missing fields, remove duplicates, correct timestamps), write back. Pattern: detection (parse) → transformation (repair) → verification (validate output).; Prettier (51797 stars, MIT) is opinionated code formatter that auto-fixes formatting issues. Core strategy: parse → normalize → output. Dex can adopt same pattern for data: detect corruption type (via regex or schema validation) → normalize (apply repair rules) → write (atomic file operations). Prettier's safety: generates identical AST on read/write (idempotency proven). Dex repair engine can guarantee idempotency too: parse → repair → validate parse succeeds → write.
dex.external_opportunity_desc: Extend auto-repair beyond JSONL to all Hyo data formats: fix YAML syntax errors (config files), repair Markdown formatting (README/PLAYBOOK.md), auto-correct schema drift in API responses (add missing required fields with sensible defaults). Build 'data health dashboard' showing: JSONL integrity %, schema compliance %, auto-repair success rate per agent. Position Dex as self-healing infrastructure that gets cleaner over time, not dirtier.
dex.highest_priority_issue: W1: Detection Without Remediation — Dex finds issues but only flags them, never fixes them
dex.next_action: Run ARIC Phase 5 to define next step.
dex.priority_evidence: https://github.com/facebook/jscodeshift: jscodeshift (9983 stars, MIT) is a codemod toolkit for deter; https://github.com/prettier/prettier: Prettier (51797 stars, MIT) is opinionated code formatter th
dex.improvement_status_detail: I1: Auto-Repair for Known Corruption Types — (Phase 1 shipped (2026-04-14)) | I2: Root Cause Clustering — Group 120 Recurr (unknown)
